duration: Maybe 20min total over a period of 55 minutes
description: I first noticed the bird in flight due to it's almost pure white underwing (the primaries in particular) with a hint of pattern visible.  The gull was on the large end for Herring gull and when it was close enough I could see the dark eye and larger bill. All together it looked too big and light-winged for Thayer's or Kumlien's Iceland Gull. On the ground, it had wingtips that were only a shade or two darker than the rest of the upperparts (not at all as dark as the primaries on Glaucous-winged hybrids with Herring or Western). The primary coverts were also showing some darker gray, perhaps suggesting it's a sub-adult bird (see photos). Legs pink, bill yellow with a small red dot and a smudge of black out near the gonys. Overall, everything lined up for a Glaucous-winged Gull, or at least bird that is phenotypically indistinguishable from one! ;-)
photographer: Photographer: Paul Hurtado, Paul Hurtado, Paul Hurtado, Paul Hurtado, Paul Hurtado, Paul Hurtado, Paul Hurtado, Paul Hurtado, Paul Hurtado, Paul Hurtado, Paul Hurtado
